name: Chocolate Silk Pudding
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Stick unsalted Butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions: This is a great recipe. It's more like a deep chocolate pudding than a bread pudding. The bread crumbs dissolve and melt into the eggs and chocolate as it bakes, forming a smooth custard that's rich but not heavy. Preheat oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit, with rack in center. Butter a 1-1/2 quart shallow baking dish. In a medium saucepan, combine the milk, butter, and chocolate. Bring to boil over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally until butter and chocolate melt. Meanwhile, in a large bowl, whisk the eggs, sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and salt. Add bread crumbs and stir in the hot milk mixture. Pour into prepared baking dish. Bake until the pudding is set and slightly wobbly but not liquid in the center, about 45-60 minutes; do not overbake. Let cool on wire rack. Serve the silk pudding slightly warm or cold, with whipped cream or vanilla ice cream if desired. Serves 6.
